card.intuition: Stack multiple sorted linked lists of decreasing density — the top level skips large blocks, lower levels refine — giving O(log n) search without the rebalancing complexity of a BST.
card.analogy: An express train system: the express stops at major cities (top level), the regional at medium towns (middle level), and the local at every station (bottom level). You take express as far as possible, then switch down.
card.time_complexity: O(log n) expected for search, insert, delete
card.space_complexity: O(n log n) expected — each element appears in O(log n) levels on average
when_to_use: Ordered key-value store where you need range queries in addition to point lookup (Redis sorted sets use skip lists)
When you want BST-like performance without rotations — skip lists are simpler to implement correctly
Concurrent data structures — skip lists admit lock-free implementations more easily than BSTs
gotchas: Level assignment must be random (coin-flip per level) — a deterministic height defeats the probabilistic balance guarantee
Maintain an update[] array during search to remember the rightmost node at each level that is less than the target — needed for O(log n) insertion
The header sentinel node has -inf key and max height — always present, never removed

exercise.prompt: Implement skip list search. Traverse from the topmost level downward, moving right while the next node's key is smaller than the target.
hints: At each level, advance right while the next node's key is strictly less than the target — stop when next would overshoot
After descending all levels, cur.next[0] is either the target node or the first node greater than target
exercise.test_code:
random.seed(42)
sl = SkipList()

def insert(sl, key, val):
    update = [None] * (MAX_LEVEL + 1)
    cur = sl.head
    for i in range(sl.level, -1, -1):
        while cur.next[i] and cur.next[i].key < key: cur = cur.next[i]
        update[i] = cur
    lvl = sl._random_level()
    if lvl > sl.level:
        for i in range(sl.level+1, lvl+1): update[i] = sl.head
        sl.level = lvl
    node = SkipNode(key, val, lvl)
    for i in range(lvl+1): node.next[i]=update[i].next[i]; update[i].next[i]=node

insert(sl,1,'a'); insert(sl,3,'c'); insert(sl,5,'e'); insert(sl,7,'g')
assert sl.search(1) == 'a', 'search(1)'
assert sl.search(3) == 'c', 'search(3)'
assert sl.search(5) == 'e', 'search(5)'
assert sl.search(2) == -1, 'search(2) missing'
assert sl.search(9) == -1, 'search(9) missing'
print('PASS')
